_doc: Owner-supplied manual player additions for THE DRAW (Ticket E0.1).
PURPOSE: the Career Path universe (football_career_paths.json) is missing players the owner
wants in the pool. Each entry names a player and asserts anchor clubs. Entries here are an
OWNER HYPOTHESIS, not a fact: Wikidata is the test. Anchors are never written to the output —
they only gate which Wikidata entity (if any) we accept. Facts still come solely from Wikidata.
SCHEMA: { name, anchorClubs: [...], ownerNote? }
name        the player, as the owner refers to them (also the entity search term)
anchorClubs clubs the owner asserts this player played for. TWO is the norm and the only
way to earn a `resolved` identity. Names are matched exactly as Career Path club
names are (strict normalized equality vs Wikidata label+altLabels, plus
clubAliases.json). National teams are NOT clubs and must never be used here.
ownerNote   why this entry looks the way it does; read it before changing an entry.
GATE (see cpRunGate in scripts/ingestWikidataPlayerData.ts):
2+ anchors, ALL confirmed in the entity's P54  => resolved
2+ anchors, ANY anchor NOT confirmed           => UNRESOLVED (reported, never forced)
exactly 1 anchor, confirmed                    => AMBER-identity, pending explicit owner ruling
candidate tie on identical anchor evidence     => UNRESOLVED (homonyms are indistinguishable)
A single anchor is deliberately NOT sufficient for `resolved`: one club cannot separate players
who share a surname and a club. That is a live risk here, not a hypothetical — see Maldini.
OWNER RULING (E1.1): { ownerRuling: { qid, verdict: "green", ruledOn, note } } resolves a
single-anchor AMBER identity. It adds NO evidence — it records that a human accepted a named
entity, and is pinned to an exact QID so the ruling can never drift onto a homonym if search
results move. The gate applies it ONLY when ownerRuling.qid equals the QID the gate itself
selected; a mismatch is warned about and ignored, never forced.

additions.name: Paolo Maldini
ownerNote: SINGLE ANCHOR BY NECESSITY, NOT BY OVERSIGHT. Paolo Maldini is a one-club player: AC Milan,
1984-2009, his entire senior career. No second club exists for the owner to supply, so the
two-anchor rule is unsatisfiable and this entry rides as AMBER-identity awaiting an explicit
owner ruling. Do NOT 'fix' this by adding a second anchor — Italy is a national team, not a
club, and Milan's youth setup is not a senior club.
WHY THAT IS RISKY HERE — VERIFIED, NOT HYPOTHETICAL. The AC Milan anchor is shared by his
father: Wikidata confirms BOTH Cesare Maldini (Q296350, b. 1932-02-05) and Paolo Maldini
(Q483027, b. 1968-06-26) have AC Milan in P54. The lone anchor therefore cannot separate
them. The gate returned a single candidate ONLY because the search term 'Paolo Maldini'
excluded Cesare — i.e. the disambiguation came from NAME similarity, which this ticket
explicitly forbids as sufficient evidence. Hence AMBER, not resolved.
OWNER RULING NEEDED on QID Q483027. Corroborating evidence (all from Wikidata, all
independently re-fetched, none of it used by the gate):
- born 1968-06-26, which is Paolo's date of birth (Cesare's is 1932-02-05)
- exactly ONE club in P54: AC Milan — consistent with the one-club career
- earliest P580 across senior clubs = 1985, consistent with Paolo's first-team debut
- position DEF, senior nation Italy
Cesare, by contrast, also played for Torino, so a Torino membership would have falsified
this candidate. To upgrade this entry to `resolved`, the gate needs a SECOND real senior
club — which does not exist for Paolo. The honest options are: accept AMBER via an explicit
owner ruling on Q483027, or leave the entry out. Do not invent an anchor to force it green.
RULED 2026-07-16 (E1.1): the owner ruled Q483027 GREEN — see ownerRuling below. The club
evidence is unchanged and still cannot separate father from son; what changed is that a
human took the decision and it is recorded as such, in both playersSourced.json and the
E1 card dossier. This is not a sourced verdict and must never be re-labelled as one.

ownerRuling.note: Owner ruled Q483027 is Paolo Maldini, not his father Cesare (Q296350). The lone AC Milan anchor cannot separate them — this is a human decision on the corroborating evidence in ownerNote (b. 1968-06-26; exactly one club in P54; earliest senior P580 1985; DEF; Italy), not new club evidence.
